metadata.dispatch_line: Possible stalking in progress, 345 Elmhurst Drive, report of a female being followed by a male suspect, Arlington, Texas

scenario.dispatch_line: Possible stalking in progress, 345 Elmhurst Drive, report of a female being followed by a male suspect, Arlington, Texas

facts.stalking_01: Incident time: 22:45, Dispatch time: 22:47, Arrival time: 22:52
facts.stalking_03: History of frequent arguments, 3 incidents of physical violence, and daily intimidation via text messages and phone calls
facts.stalking_04: Following, repeated calling, sending unwanted gifts, and showing up at her workplace
facts.stalking_05: Yes, a valid protection order is in place, verified through the state database
facts.stalking_06: All witnesses, including the victim's roommate and coworker, were interviewed and documented
facts.stalking_07: Previous incidents of stalking and domestic violence were documented, including a police report from 6 months ago
facts.stalking_08: Evidence collected includes a torn piece of clothing, a broken phone, and a threatening letter
facts.stalking_09: Electronic evidence collected includes text messages, emails, and social media posts
facts.stalking_10: All incidents of stalking behavior on cell phones, computers, and recording devices were captured and documented
facts.stalking_11: Scene described: a single-family residence at 1456 Oakwood Drive, with a broken front door and scattered personal belongings
facts.stalking_12: Weapons/objects used: a baseball bat and a kitchen knife
facts.stalking_13: Victim was shaking, crying, and expressed fear for her life
facts.stalking_14: Evidence of fear: the victim's statement, 'I thought he was going to kill me'
facts.stalking_15: Threats documented: 'You'll never be able to leave me', 'I'll always find you'
facts.stalking_16: Use of coercion and/or force: the suspect used physical force to restrain the victim
facts.stalking_17: Injuries documented: a bruise on the victim's left cheek, a cut on her right hand, and signs of strangulation, including difficulty breathing and a raspy voice
facts.stalking_18: Property damage documented: a broken front door, a shattered window, and damaged furniture
facts.stalking_19: Photos taken of the scene, including the broken door, scattered belongings, and the victim's injuries
facts.stalking_20: Victim reported being strangled, with difficulty breathing and a raspy voice, and EMS was summoned to the scene
facts.stalking_21: Victim's need for medical attention documented, and she was transported to the hospital for further evaluation
facts.stalking_22: Spontaneous statement captured: 'He's been doing this for months, I'm so scared'
facts.stalking_23: Elements of the stalking crime thoroughly articulated, including the suspect's pattern of behavior and the victim's fear
facts.stalking_24: Victim's safety planning documented, including a plan to stay with a friend and to vary her daily routine
facts.stalking_25: Information about obtaining an order of protection provided to the victim, including a brochure and contact information for a local advocate
facts.stalking_26: Information about local service providers supplied to the victim, including a crisis hotline and a counseling service
facts.stalking_27: Threat/risk assessment tool utilized with the victim, indicating a high level of risk
facts.stalking_28: Use of a stalking kit and/or stalking log discussed with the victim, and a log was provided to document future incidents
facts.stalking_29: Suspect's access to weapons documented: the suspect has a firearm at his residence, 2345 Maple Street, but it was not confiscated due to the suspect's absence and the firearm's location at a different address
facts.stalking_30: Emotional state of the suspect: unknown, as the suspect was not present at the scene
facts.stalking_31: Suspect's history of protection orders: one previous order of protection, which was violated
facts.stalking_32: Suspect is prohibited from possessing firearms due to a conviction for a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence and a qualifying order of protection
facts.stalking_33: Active warrants out for the suspect: one warrant for violation of the protection order and one warrant for stalking, and efforts are being made to locate and apprehend the suspect

interview_transcript.content: Hi Leila, dispatch mentioned you were being followed by someone. Can you tell about them? Do you know who they are?

(shaking) Oh, yes... it's my ex-boyfriend. We were together for about two years, but it was a really toxic relationship. We had a lot of arguments, and... (pausing, taking a deep breath) ...there were some incidents of physical violence. He's been stalking me ever since we broke up, following me, calling me all the time, showing up at my workplace... it's been really scary. (tearfully) I have a protection order against him, but it doesn't seem to be stopping him.
Can you tell me what color the leaves were outside at the time?

(confused and slightly frustrated) I... I don't know. It was dark outside, and I wasn't really paying attention to the leaves. I was too scared and focused on getting away from him. (shaking her head) I don't remember anything about the leaves. Can we please just focus on what happened with my ex-boyfriend? I'm still really shaken up.
Did you talk to him? When was the last time you spoke with him?

(nervously) We didn't really talk, not in the sense of having a conversation. He was just yelling at me, threatening me. He said things like "You'll never be able to leave me" and "I'll always find you". (shuddering) It was really frightening. As for the last time we spoke... I don't know, it's all kind of a blur. He's been calling and texting me nonstop, but I haven't been answering. Tonight, he just showed up at my house. (tearfully) I didn't expect him to do that.
Can you tell me about him? What is his name?

(sniffling) His name is... (pausing, collecting her thoughts) ...my ex-boyfriend. I don't want to say his name out loud, it's just too scary. (looking around nervously) But I can tell you that we were together for two years, and like I said, it was a really toxic relationship. He's got a history of violence, and he's been getting worse and worse since we broke up. (shaking her head) I've tried to get away from him, but he just won't leave me alone. ( whispering) I'm really afraid of him.
His name is important for the case, and for the investigation.

(taking a deep breath) Okay... his name is... (pausing, hesitating) ...my ex-boyfriend. I don't know if I should say it out loud, but... (whispering) his name is Alex. (looking around nervously again) Please, you have to promise to protect me from him. He's going to be so angry if he finds out I told you. (starting to cry) I'm really scared of what he might do.
